sections.heading: Alert Conditions
sections.body:
You can configure the following alert conditions in the project-level
[Configure Alert Settings](configure-alerts) page to trigger alerts.

The [Disk read IOPS on Data Partition is](<Disk read IOPS on Data Partition is>) alert condition is
raised if the average number of disk read operations per second exceeds
the specified threshold.

The [Disk write IOPS on Data Partition is](<Disk write IOPS on Data Partition is>) alert condition is
raised if the average number of disk write operations per second
exceeds the specified threshold.

[System: CPU (User) % is](<System: CPU (User) % is>) measures the CPU usage of the
processes on the node, normalized by the number of CPUs. This value is
scaled to a range of 0-100%.

sections.heading: Common Triggers
sections.body:
A few common events may trigger these alerts:

- [Unoptimized queries](manual/core/query-optimization).

- A one-time event, such as an [index build](manual/core/index-creation).

sections.heading: Fix the Immediate Problem
sections.body:
Consider a few possible actions to help resolve
these alerts:

- [Optimize your queries](manual/core/query-optimization).

- Use the Atlas Performance Advisor
  to view slow queries and suggested [indexes](manual/indexes).

- Review [Indexing Strategies](manual/applications/indexes) for
  possible further indexing improvements.

  > **Note:** You might need to temporarily increase your cluster IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) to
  > create new indexes. Provisioned IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) is supported only for
  > `M30+` tier AWS (Amazon Web Services) clusters.

To change a cluster's IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second), go to the
**Cluster Configuration** page and do the following possible
actions:

| Cloud Provider            | Tier          | Possible Actions                                                 |
| ------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| AWS (Amazon Web Services) | M10, M20      | Increase Storage CapacityScale Cluster Tier                      |
| AWS (Amazon Web Services) | M30 or larger | Increase Cluster IOPSIncrease Storage CapacityScale Cluster Tier |
| Google Cloud              | M10 or larger | Increase Storage CapacityScale Cluster Tier                      |
| Azure                     | M10 or larger | Increase Storage CapacityScale Cluster Tier                      |

- [Analyze Query Performance](manual/tutorial/analyze-query-plan)
  to review how your queries are using your indexes.

- Increase hardware resources, such as instance size and IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second), in the
  Cluster Configuration Page.

sections.heading: Disk IOPS Burst Credits for Atlas Clusters on Azure (Microsoft Azure)
sections.body:
Atlas clusters deployed to Azure (Microsoft Azure) may use credit-based
bursting, but the disk will burst only if it has burst credits
accumulated in its credit bucket. Azure (Microsoft Azure) also offers an on-demand
bursting model, where the disk bursts whenever its needs exceed its
current capacity.

See the Azure (Microsoft Azure) [Disk Bursting documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disk-bursting) for more information about how bursting
for Azure (Microsoft Azure) disks works.

sections.heading: Conserve Burst Credits
sections.body:
If you regularly exceed your configured IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) threshold, you can avoid
depleting your burst credits by increasing your configured IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) with
any of the following actions:

- [Modify a Cluster](scale-cluster-storage) to increase the
  cluster's IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) threshold.

- [Modify a Cluster](scale-cluster-storage). The
  IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) threshold increases as you increase storage capacity. Clusters
  configured to use at least 1 TB of storage have baseline IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second)
  performance that is equal to or greater than the maximum burst
  performance. These volumes do not deplete burst credit balances.

- [Modify the Cluster Tier](scale-cluster-instance). Larger cluster
  tiers include higher IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) thresholds.

  > **Note:** Cluster tiers `M140` and larger are deployed with at least 1 TB
  > of storage capacity by default. Clusters with 1 TB or more of
  > storage capacity do not deplete burst credit balances.

sections.heading: Monitor Your Progress
sections.body:
These are a few possible methods to observe high disk I/O:

- The disk IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) use from the **Disk IOPS** graph in the
  cluster metrics exceeds the
  provisioned IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) from the Atlas cluster
  configuration page.

- The **Normalized System CPU**
  metric
  has a high **IOWait** curve. IOWait measures the percentage
  of time the CPU is idle and waiting for an I/O operation to complete.
  The **Normalized System CPU** chart is located under the
  **Hardware Metrics** section of the **Metrics** tab.
